author: John Chrysostom

classification.cautionReason: No major mainstream heresy warning is attached by the preview classifier.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: “They say unto the blind man again, What sayest thou of him, that he hath opened thine eyes?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: He said, He is a Prophet.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: The Jews then did not believe.”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: We must go over the Scriptures not in a chance way or carelessly, but with all exactness, that we be not entangled.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: Since even now in this place one might with show of reason question, how, when they had asserted, “This man is not of God, because he keepeth not the Sabbath,” they now say to the man, “What sayest thou of him, that he hath opened thine eyes?” and not, “What sayest thou of him, that he hath broken the Sabbath?” but put now that which was the ground of the defense, not that of the accusation.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: What then have we to reply?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: That these (who speak) are not the men who said, “This man is not of God,” but those who separated themselves from them, who also said, “A man that is a sinner cannot do such miracles.”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: For desiring to silence their opponents the more, in order that they may not seem to be partisans of Christ, they bring forward the man who had received proof of His power, and question him.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: Observe now the wisdom of the poor man, he speaketh more wisely than them all.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: First he saith, “He is a Prophet”; and shrank not from the judgment of the perverse Jews who spake against Him and said, “How can this man be of God, not keeping the Sabbath?” but replied to them, “He is a Prophet.”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: “And they did not believe that he had been blind, and received his sight, until they had called his parents.”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: Observe in how many ways they attempt to obscure and take away the miracle.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: But this is the nature of truth, by the very means by which it seems to be assailed by men, by these it becomes stronger, it shines by means of that by which it is obscured.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: For if these things had not taken place, the miracle might have been suspected by the many; but now, as if desiring to lay bare the truth, so do they use all means, and would not have acted otherwise, supposing they had done all in Christ’s behalf.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: For they first attempted to cast Him down by occasion of this mode (of cure), saying, “How opened he thine eyes?” that is, “was it by some sorcery?”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: In another place also, when they had no charge to bring against Him, they endeavored to insult the mode of the cure, saying, “He doth not cast out devils save by Beelzebub.” ( Matt. xii.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: And here again, when they have nothing to say, they betake themselves to the time (of cure), saying, “He breaketh the Sabbath”; and again, “He is a sinner.”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: Yet He asked you, who would slay Him, and who were ready to lay hold of His actions, most plainly, saying, “Which of you convinceth Me of sin?” ( c. viii.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: 46 ); and no man spake, nor said “Thou blasphemest because thou makest thyself without sin.”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: But if they had had it in their power to say so, they would not have held their peace.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: For they who because they heard that He was before Abraham would have stoned Him, and said that He was not of God, who boasted that they, murderers as they were, were of God, but who said that One who did such wonders, after that He had wrought a cure, was not of God, because He kept not the Sabbath, if they had had but a shadow of a charge against Him, would never have let it pass.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: And if they call Him a sinner because He seemed to break the Sabbath, this charge also is shown to be unsound, when those who are ranked with them condemn their great coldness and littleness of soul.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: Being therefore entangled on every side, they afterwards betake themselves to something else more shameless and impudent.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: What is that?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: They “did not believe,” It saith, “that he had been blind, and received his sight.”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: How then did they charge Christ with not keeping the Sabbath?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: Plainly, as having believed.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: But why gave ye not heed to the great number of people? to the neighbors who knew him?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: As I said, falsehood everywhere defeats itself by the very means by which it seems to annoy the truth, and makes the truth to appear more bright.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: Which was now the case.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: For that no one might say that his neighbors and those who had seen him did not speak with precision, but guessed from a likeness, they bring forward his parents, by whom they succeeded against their will in proving that what had taken place was real, since the parents best of all knew their own child.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: When they could not terrify the man himself, but beheld him with all boldness proclaim his Benefactor, they thought to wound the miracle by means of his parents.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: Observe the malice of their questioning.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: For what saith it?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: Having placed them in the midst so as to throw them into distress, they apply the questioning with great severity and anger,

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: “Is this your son?” (and they said not, “who once was blind,” but) “of whom ye say that he was born blind?”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: As if they were acting deceitfully, and plotting on behalf of Christ.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: O ye accursed, utterly accursed!

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: What father would choose to invent such falsehoods against his child?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: For they almost say, “Whom ye have made out blind, and not only so, but have spread abroad the report everywhere.”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: “How then doth he now see?”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: “Yours,” saith one, “is the trick and the contrivance.”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: For by these two things do they attempt to lead the parents to a denial; by using the words, “Whom ye say,” and, “How then doth he now see?”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: Now when there were three questions asked, whether he was their son, whether he had been blind, and how he received his sight, the parents only acknowledged two of them, but do not add the third.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: And this came to pass for the sake of the truth, in order that none other save the man that was healed, who was also worthy of credit, should acknowledge this matter.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: And how would the parents have favored (Christ), when even of what they knew some part they spake not through fear of the Jews?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: What say they?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: “We know that this is our son, and that he was born blind; but by what means he now seeth we know not, or who hath opened his eyes we know not; he is of age, he shall speak for himself.”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: By making him to be worthy of credit, they begged off themselves; “He is not a child, say they, nor incapable, but able to testify for himself.”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: “These words spake they, because they feared the Jews.”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: Observe how the Evangelist again brings forward their opinion and thoughts.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: This I say, because of that speech which they before uttered, when they said, “He maketh Himself equal to God.” ( c. v.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: For had that also been the opinion of the Jews but not the judgment of Christ, he would have added and said, that “it was a Jewish opinion.”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: When therefore the parents referred them to him that had been healed, they called him again the second time, and did not say openly and shamelessly, “Deny that Christ healed thee,” but would fain effect this under a pretense of piety.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: “Give,” saith one, “the glory to God.”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: For to have said to the parents, “Deny that he is your son, and that he was born blind,” would have seemed very ridiculous.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: And again, to have said this to himself would have been manifest shamelessness.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: Wherefore they say not so, but manage the matter in another way, saying, “Give God the glory,” that is, “confess that this man hath wrought nothing.”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: “We know that this man is a sinner.”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: “Why then did ye not convict Him when He said, ‘Which of you convinceth Me of sin?’ ( c. viii.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: Whence know ye that He is a sinner?”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: After that they had said, “Give God the glory,” and the man had made no reply, Christ meeting praised him, and did not rebuke him, nor say, “Wherefore hast thou not given glory to God?”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: But what said He?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: “Dost thou believe on the Son of God?” ( ver.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: 35 ), that thou mayest learn that this is “to give glory to God.”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: Now had He not been equal in honor to the Father, this would not have been giving glory; but since he that honoreth the Son honoreth the Father also, the blind is with good reason not rebuked.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: Now while they expected that the parents would contradict and deny the miracle, the Pharisees said nothing to the man himself, but when they saw that they profited nothing by this, they again return to him, saying, “This man is a sinner.”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: “He answered and said, Whether he be a sinner or no, I know not; one thing I know, that, whereas I was blind, now I see.”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: Surely the blind man was not terrified?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: That be far from him.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: How then doth he who said, “He is a Prophet” ( ver.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: 17 ), now say, “Whether he be a sinner, I know not”?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: He said so, not as being in such a state of mind, nor as having persuaded himself of this thing, but desiring to clear Him from their charges by the testimony of the fact, not by his own declaration, and to make the defense credible, when the testimony of the good deed done should decide the matter against them.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: Since if after many words when the blind man said, “Except this were a righteous man he could not do such miracles” ( ver.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: 33 ), they were so enraged as to reply, “Thou wast altogether born in sin, and dost thou teach us?” what would they not have said, if he had spoken so from the beginning; what would they not have done?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: “Whether he be a sinner or not, I know not”; as though he had said, “I say nothing in this man’s favor, I make no declaration at present, yet this I certainly know and would affirm, that if he were a sinner he could not have done such things.”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: Thus he kept himself free from suspicion, and his testimony uncorrupted, as not speaking from partiality, but as bearing witness according to the fact.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: When therefore they could neither upset nor remove what had been done, they again return to their former plan, making trifling enquiries about the manner of the cure, like men who search on every side about a prey which is before them, and cannot be hurt, hastening round now in one direction, now in another; and they recur to the man’s former assertions, in order now to make them unsound by continual questions, and say,

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: “What did he to thee?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: How opened he thine eyes?”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: What was his reply?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: Having conquered and cast them down, he no longer speaks to them submissly.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: As long as the matter needed enquiry and arguments he spake guardedly, while he supplied the proof; but when he had conquered and gained a splendid victory, he then takes courage, and tramples upon them.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: What saith he?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: “I have told you once, and ye did not hear; wherefore would ye hear it again?”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: Seest thou the bold-speaking of a beggar towards Scribes and Pharisees?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: So strong is truth, so weak is falsehood.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: Truth, though she take hold but of ordinary men, maketh them to appear glorious; the other, even though it be with the strong, shows them weak.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: What he saith is of this kind: “Ye give no heed to my words, therefore I will no longer speak or answer you continually, who question me to no purpose, and who do not desire to hear in order to learn, but that you may insult over my words.”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: “Will ye also be His disciples?”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: Now he hath ranked himself among the band of disciples, for the “will ye also?” is the expression of one who is declaring himself to be a disciple.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: Then he mocked and annoyed them abundantly.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: For since he knew that this struck them hard, he said it, wishing to upbraid them with exceeding severity; the act of a soul courageous, soaring on high and despising their madness, pointing out the greatness of this dignity, in which he was very confident, and showing that they insulted him who was a man worthy to be admired, but that he took not the insult to himself, but grasped as an honor what they offered as a reproach.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: “Thou art his disciple, but we are Moses’ disciples.”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: “But this cannot be.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: Ye are neither Moses’ nor this Man’s; for were ye Moses’, ye would become this Man’s also.”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: Wherefore Christ before said unto them, because they were continually betaking themselves to these speeches, “Had ye believed Moses, ye would have believed Me, for he wrote of Me.” ( c. v.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: “We know that God spake unto Moses.”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: By whose word, whose report?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: “That of our forefathers,” saith one.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: Is not He then more to be believed than your forefathers, who confirmeth by miracles that He came from God, and that He speaketh things from above?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: They said not, “We have heard that God spake to Moses,” but, “We know.”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: Do ye affirm, O Jews, what ye have by hearing, as knowing it, but deem what ye have by sight as less certain than what ye have by hearing?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: Yet the one ye saw not, but heard, the other ye did not hear, but saw.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: What then saith the blind man?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: “Why herein is a marvelous thing, that ye know not whence He is, and He doeth such miracles.”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: “That a Man, who is not one of the distinguished or noble or illustrious among you, can do such things; so that it is in every way clear that He is God, needing no human aid.”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: “We know that God heareth not sinners.”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: Since they had been the first to say, “How can a man that is a sinner do such miracles?” ( ver.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: 16 ), he now brings forward even their judgment, reminding them of their own words.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: “This opinion,” saith he, “is common to me and you.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: Stand fast now to it.”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: And observe, I pray you, his wisdom.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: He turns about the miracle in every way, because they could not do away with it, and from it he draws his inferences.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: Seest thou that at first he said “Whether he be a sinner or not, I know not”? not doubting (God forbid!) but knowing that He was not a sinner.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: At least now, when he had an opportunity, see how he defended Him.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: “We know that God heareth not sinners”:

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: “But if any man be a worshiper of God, and doeth His will.”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: Here he not only hath cleared Him from sin, but declareth that He is very pleasing to God, and doeth all His will.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: For since they called themselves worshipers of God, he added, “and doeth His will”; “since,” saith he, “it is not sufficient to know God: men must also do His will.”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: Then he magnifies what had been done, saying,

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: “Since the world began was it not heard that any man opened the eyes of one that was born blind.”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: “If now ye acknowledge that God heareth not sinners, and this Person hath wrought a miracle, and such a miracle as no man ever wrought, it is clear that He hath surpassed all things in virtue, and that His power is greater than belongeth to man.”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: What then say they?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: “Thou wast altogether born in sins, and dost thou teach us?”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: As long as they expected that he would deny Christ, they deemed him trustworthy, calling upon him once and a second time.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: If ye deemed him not trustworthy, why did ye call and question him a second time?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: But when he spake the truth, unabashed, then, when they ought most to have admired, they condemned him.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: But what is the, “Thou wast altogether born in sins”?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: They here unsparingly reproach him with his very blindness, as though they had said, “Thou art in sins from thy earliest age;” insinuating that on this account he was born blind; which was contrary to reason.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: On this point at least Christ comforting him said, “For judgment I am come into the world, that they which see not might see, and that they which see might be made blind.” ( c. ix.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: “Thou wast altogether born in sins, and dost thou teach us?”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: Why, what had the man said?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: Did he set forth his private opinion?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: Did he not set forth a common judgment, saying, “We know that God heareth not sinners”?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: Did he not produce your own words?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: “And they cast him out.”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: Hast thou beheld the herald of the truth, how poverty was no hindrance to his true wisdom?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: Seest thou what reproaches, what sufferings he bare from the beginning, and how by word and by deed he testified?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: Now these things are recorded, that we too may imitate them.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: For if the blind man, the beggar, who had not even seen Him, straightway showed such boldness even before he was encouraged by Christ, standing opposed to a whole people, murderous, possessed, and raving, who desired by means of his voice to condemn Christ, if he neither yielded nor gave back, but most boldly stopped their mouths, and chose rather to be cast out than to betray the truth; how much more ought we, who have lived so long in the faith, who have seen ten thousand marvels wrought by faith, who have received greater benefits than he, have recovered the sight of the eyes within, have beheld the ineffable Mysteries, and have been called to such honor, how ought we, I say, to exhibit all boldness of speech towards those who attempt to accuse, and who say anything against the Christians, and to stop their mouths, and not to acquiesce without an effort.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: And we shall be able to do this, if we are bold, and give heed to the Scriptures, and hear them not carelessly.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: For if one should come in here regularly, even though he read not at home, if he attends to what is said here, one year even is sufficient to make him well versed in them; because we do not to-day read one kind of Scriptures, and tomorrow another, but always and continually the same.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: Still such is the wretched disposition of the many, that after so much reading, they do not even know the names of the Books, and are not ashamed nor tremble at entering so carelessly into a place where they may hear God’s word.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: Yet if a harper, or dancer, or stage-player call the city, they all run eagerly, and feel obliged to him for the call, and spend the half of an entire day in attending to him alone; but when God speaketh to us by Prophets and Apostles, we yawn, we scratch ourselves, we are drowsy.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: And in summer, the heat seems too great, and we betake ourselves to the market place; and again, in winter, the rain and mire are a hindrance, and we sit at home; yet at horse races, though there is no roof over them to keep off the wet, the greater number, while heavy rains are falling, and the wind is dashing the water into their faces, stand like madmen, caring not for cold, and wet, and mud, and length of way, and nothing either keeps them at home, or prevents their going thither.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: But here, where there are roofs over head, and where the warmth is admirable, they hold back instead of running together; and this too, when the gain is that of their own souls.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: How is this tolerable, tell me?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: Thus it happens, that while we are more skilled than any in those matters, in things necessary we are more ignorant than children.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: If a man call you a charioteer, or a dancer, you say that you have been insulted, and use every means to wipe off the affront; but if he draw you to be a spectator of the action, you do not start away, and the art whose name you shun, you almost in every case pursue.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: But where you ought to have both the action and the name, both to be and to be called a Christian, you do not even know what kind of thing the action is.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: What can be worse than this folly?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: These things I have desired continually to say to you, but I fear lest I gain hatred in vain and unprofitably.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: For I perceive that not only the young are mad, but the old also; about whom I am especially ashamed, when I see a man venerable from his white hairs, disgracing those white hairs, and drawing a child after him.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: What is worse than this mockery?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: What more shameful than this conduct?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: The child is taught by the father to act unseemly.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: Do the words sting?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: This is what I desire, that you should suffer the pain caused by the words, in order to be delivered from the disgrace caused by the actions.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: For there are some too far colder than these, who are not even ashamed at the things spoken of, nay, who even put together a long argument in defense of the action.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: If you ask them who was Amos or Obadiah, or what is the number of the Prophets or Apostles, they cannot even open their mouth but for horses and charioteers, they compose excuses more cleverly than sophists or rhetoricians, and after all this, they say, “What is the harm? what is the loss?”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: This is what I groan for, that ye do not so much as know that the action is a loss, nor have a sense of its evils.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: God hath given to thee an appointed space of life for serving Him, and dost thou while thou spendest it vainly, and at random, and on nothing useful, still ask, “What loss is there?”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: If thou hast spent a little money to no purpose, thou callest it a loss: when thou spendest whole days of thine upon the devil’s pageants, thinkest thou that thou art doing nothing wrong?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: Thou oughtest to spend all thy life in supplications and prayers, whereas thou wastest thy life and substance heedlessly, and to thine own hurt, on shouts, and uproar, and shameful words, and fighting, and unseasonable pleasure, and actions performed by trickery, and after all this thou askest, “What is the loss?” not knowing thou shouldest be lavish of anything rather than time.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: Gold, if thou shalt have spent, thou mayest get again; but if thou lose time, thou shalt hardly recover that.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: Little is dealt out to us in this present life; if therefore we employ it not as we ought, what shall we say when we depart “there”?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: For tell me, if thou hadst commanded one of thy sons to learn some art, and then he had continually stayed at home, or even passed his time somewhere else, would not the teacher reject him?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: Would he not say to thee, “Thou hast made an agreement with me, and appointed a time; if now thy son will not spend this time with me but in other places, how shall I produce him to thee as a scholar?”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: Thus also we must speak.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: For God will say also to us, “I gave you time to learn this art of piety, wherefore have ye foolishly and uselessly wasted that time?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: Why did ye neither go constantly to the teacher, nor give heed to his words?”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: For to show that piety is an art, hear what the Prophet saith, “Come, ye children, hearken unto me; I will teach you the fear of the Lord.” ( Ps. xxxiv.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: And again, “Blessed is the man whom Thou instructest, Lord, and teachest him out of Thy Law.” ( Ps. xciv.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: When therefore thou hast spent this time in vain, what excuse wilt thou have?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: “And why,” saith some one, “did He deal out to us but little time?”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: O senselessness and ingratitude!

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: That for which thou wert most bounden to give thanks to Him, for that He hath cut short thy labors and abridged thy toils, and made the rest long and everlasting, for this dost thou find fault, and art discontented?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: But I know not how we have brought our discourse to this point, and have made it so long; we must therefore shorten it now.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: For this too is a part of our wretchedness, that here if the discourse be long, we all become careless, while there they begin at noon, and retire by torch and lamp light.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: However, that we be not always chiding, we now entreat and beseech you, grant this favor to us and to yourselves; and getting free from all other matters, to these let us rivet ourselves.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: So shall we gain from you joy and gladness, and honor on your account, and a recompense for these labors; while ye will reap all the reward, because having been aforetime so madly riveted to the stage, ye tore yourselves away, through fear of God, and by our exhortations, from that malady, and brake your bonds, and hastened unto God.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: Nor is it “there” alone that ye shall receive your reward, but “here” also ye shall enjoy pure pleasure.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: Such a thing is virtue; besides giving us crowns in heaven, even here it maketh life pleasant to us.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: Let us then be persuaded by what has been said, that we may obtain the blessings both here and hereafter, through the grace and lovingkindness of our Lord Jesus Christ, by whom and with whom, to the Father and the Holy Ghost be glory, now and ever and world without end.
